recommended_paths: Add a served-bundle runtime probe that starts on anonymous Home, proves no automatic promotion/open-app handoff occurs, and then exercises one protected action that opens the canonical app-promotion gate without mutation side effects.
exact_next_step: Record reviewer resolutions in the governing TODO using the machine-checkable resolution table or equivalent gate ledger, then extract the derived resolution packet and decide whether another bounded review pass is still required.

findings.title: Served-bundle runtime proof is still too map-specific to cover the absorbed Home startup contract
findings.rationale: The bounded package includes a served-bundle proof for permission-granted map entry, but the absorbed startup rule also claims anonymous Home remains public and only guarded routes or actions should trigger the canonical promotion surface. Without a browser/runtime proof for that exact cold-start contract, a startup regression could slip through while the map probe remains green.
findings.suggested_action: Capture a served-bundle runtime artifact proving anonymous Home cold start stays public with no `/open-app`, no automatic promotion UI, no protected-read failures, and one representative guarded action that opens the canonical app-promotion surface without favorite mutation.
